title: Pipeline Lexicon
description: Schema for pipeline lexicon definition files. A lexicon defines the status vocabulary for a delivery type: valid statuses, allowed transitions, directory-to-status mappings, actionable states, metadata auto-population rules, and an optional derivation hook.

$schema.description: Reference to this schema file for editor validation.

extends.description: Lexicon ID of the parent to inherit from (e.g. 'soc._base'). Child keys override parent; nested objects merge recursively. Max inheritance depth is 3.

statuses.description: Valid status values for this delivery type (e.g. ['pending', 'passed', 'failed']).

transitions.description: Allowed status transitions. Keys are source statuses, values are arrays of valid target statuses. An empty array means no transitions are allowed from that status.

dir_map.description: Maps terminal directory names to statuses (e.g. {'msoc': 'passed', 'msoc_new': 'pending'}). The crawler uses this to derive a delivery's initial status from its directory path.

actionable_statuses.description: Statuses that indicate a delivery is ready for downstream processing (e.g. conversion).

metadata_fields.description: Named metadata fields that can be auto-populated on status transitions. Keys are field names (e.g. 'passed_at'), values define the field type and trigger.

derive_hook.description: Python import path for a status derivation function, in 'module.path:function' format. The function signature is (deliveries: list[ParsedDelivery], lexicon: Lexicon) -> list[ParsedDelivery].

type.description: Data type of the metadata field.

set_on.description: Status that triggers auto-population of this field. When a delivery transitions to this status, the field is set automatically (datetime fields get UTC ISO timestamp).
